source_jobs.title: Founding Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LELAND CARE

The typical US senior sees seven physicians across four practices plus insurers, pharmacies, family members, and facility staff. No one has the full picture. Fragmented data in incompatible systems leaves seniors and families managing chaos instead of focusing on living well.

Leland is building the first unified system of record for aging to help seniors live longer, healthier, and happier lives. With one complete record of a senior’s health and life, AI can intelligently analyze the full picture to surface risks, gaps, and opportunities, while the senior’s trusted human navigator guides the next best actions and removes friction so families can actually follow through. This turns fragmented information into proactive, coordinated support that extends healthy, independent years.

We have a multi-year funding runway and are launching pilots in 2026 with providers and already in active conversations with major US health systems. The company is led by a Stanford GSB founder with experience scaling new businesses at Sword Health and Homebase.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Founding Engineer, you are our first engineer and the technical leader. You will build the core platform from scratch, owning architecture, data systems, ingestion pipelines, the intelligence layer, and the initial agentic systems that turn a complete record of a senior’s health and life into coordinated action.

We operate through four layers:

 - Ingest & Store: Pull and structure data from across the healthcare system and combine it with real context on family, caretakers, medications, appointments, and daily life. The goal is one clean, reliable record instead of scattered fragments.

 - Intelligence: Use AI to continuously analyze the full picture, detect gaps and risks, and prioritize what actually matters.

 - Action: Build agents that execute real coordination work (scheduling, logistics, reconciliation, documentation) while keeping the human navigator in the loop with full visibility.

 - Compliance: Make sure every action is properly documented and auditable so the system can scale responsibly.

Expect to make high-leverage technical decisions, ship fast, and own outcomes end-to-end with minimal process. This is a true 0-1 role. You will define how the platform works.





WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - You have built real systems from scratch and take extreme ownership of problems end-to-end.

 - High agency. You figure things out, move fast, and do whatever it takes to make progress. You don’t wait for perfect requirements or perfect conditions.

 - You thrive in ambiguity and chaos. You prefer clear goals and maximum freedom over hand-holding and process.

 - You are genuinely excited to work on hard problems that matter. Fixing broken parts of healthcare for the people who need it most is meaningful to you.

 - First-principles thinker with strong technical judgment. You make pragmatic architecture decisions and ship high-quality work quickly.

 - Low ego, high output. You want the intensity and ownership that only exists at the earliest stage of a company.

If you need constant direction, clear roadmaps, or a well-defined role, this is not the right place.

source_jobs.title: Head of Operations
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LELAND CARE

The typical US senior sees seven physicians across four practices plus insurers, pharmacies, family members, and facility staff. No one has the full picture. Fragmented data in incompatible systems leaves seniors and families managing chaos instead of focusing on living well.

Leland is building the first unified system of record for aging to help seniors live longer, healthier, and happier lives. With one complete record of a senior’s health and life, AI can intelligently analyze the full picture to surface risks, gaps, and opportunities, while the senior’s trusted human navigator guides the next best actions and removes friction so families can actually follow through. This turns fragmented information into proactive, coordinated support that extends healthy, independent years.

We have a multi-year funding runway and are launching pilots in 2026 with providers and already in active conversations with major US health systems. The company is led by a Stanford GSB founder with experience scaling new businesses at Sword Health and Homebase.



ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Founding Head of Operations, you are the operating backbone of the company. You turn strategy into execution and you build the machine that lets a regulated healthcare company move fast without breaking. One day you are running a special project or prepping the board, the next you are standing up our billing rails or fixing a broken process before it scales.

You will own four things:

 - Force multiplier to the CEO: Run special projects end-to-end, drive hiring and onboarding, support fundraising and board prep, and own the dozens of cross-functional threads that keep the company moving.

 - Compliance and billing rails: Stand up the infrastructure that makes us legitimate and reimbursable. HIPAA, BAA, OIG review, SOC 2, entity and TIN, Medicare enrollment, consent and copay workflows, and audit-ready documentation.

 - Revenue cycle: Run the rails day to day. Time tracking, claims submission, acceptance, denials, and days to cash. Turn delivered navigation into clean, collected revenue.

 - Analytics and reporting: Build the reporting backbone. Track outcomes and leading indicators, run the weekly cadence, and give the team and the board a clear, honest picture of what is working.

Expect to make high-leverage operational decisions, move fast, and own outcomes end-to-end with minimal process. This is a true 0-1 role. You will define how the company runs.



WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - You are a generalist operator who goes deep. You can run a fundraising workstream and a claims workflow in the same week and do both well.

 - You have hands-on clinical billing experience. You have personally run Medicare, Medicaid, or commercial billing, documentation, credentialing, and denials, and know what breaks and how to stay compliant.

 - High agency. You figure things out, move fast, and do whatever it takes to make progress. You don’t wait for perfect requirements or perfect conditions.

 - You thrive in ambiguity and chaos. You prefer clear goals and maximum freedom over hand-holding and process.

 - You bring structure to messes. You see the broken process or the missing system before it becomes a fire, and you build the fix.

 - Low ego, high output. You want the intensity and ownership that only exists at the earliest stage of a company.

If you need constant direction, clear roadmaps, or a well-defined role, this is not the right place.

source_jobs.title: Entrepreneur-in-Residence (EIR)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Terrarium https://www.terrarium.vc/ is a digital health venture studio backed by Company Ventures https://www.companyventures.com/, an early stage venture firm with a 10 year history building and operating NYC’s premier venture community and founder residency, Grand Central Tech. Company Ventures currently manages ~$150mm in AUM across its early stage Fund I (2018), Fund II (2022), and growth investment vehicles. Notable healthcare investments include: Maven, Centivo, Season, Particle, Stepful, Eden Health, Nomad, Octave, Elaborate, Hero, Nara, Reverence Care, Oova, and more.

Terrarium incubates high conviction, transformational healthcare companies. We accelerate the startup journey for the best early stage founders with world class investment research, pre-seed capital, and enterprise partnerships. We are a talent obsessed studio and this position is central to Terrarium's overall strategy.




THE OPPORTUNITY

As an EIR at Terrarium you will partner with our team to validate a high conviction thesis, pressure test GTM and unit economics, build the early product path, and recruit the first teammates. You will use our research stack, customer network, and capital pathway to turn insight into a venture-backable company.




WHAT YOU WILL DO:

 - Lead a zero-to-one company creation process from problem discovery to a backable plan

 - Run rapid experiments that validate customer pain, willingness to pay, and repeatable acquisition

 - Translate insights into a clear product spec and near-term roadmap, then ship an MVP

 - Build the early operating model including pricing, GTM motion, and success metrics

 - Engage Terrarium’s provider and payer partners to secure pilots and letters of intent

 - Recruit and evaluate cofounders and founding hires through Terrarium’s cofounder matching network

 - Prepare for investment committee with crisp narrative, milestones, and capital plan
   
   


THIS ROLE IS RIGHT FOR YOU IF….

 - You have founder energy and bias to action, with a record of building or owning zero-to-one work

 - You know healthcare systems or health tech and can navigate clinical, regulatory, and data realities

 - You are commercial by default and comfortable building in market with customers

 - You write clearly, make decisions with incomplete data, and love fast feedback loops

 - You enjoy recruiting exceptional people and setting a high performance bar




WHAT YOU BRING:

 - 6 to 12+ years in startups, product, engineering, operations, or new business building

 - Evidence of shipped product, launched services, or scaled programs that drove revenue or outcomes

 - Strength in one or more of: product management, full-stack or data engineering, B2B SaaS GTM, healthcare operations, analytics, or marketplace design

 - Customer research fluency and a metrics mindset

 - Integrity, resilience, and a collaborative style




WHY WE ARE EXCITED ABOUT WE ARE BUILDING:

 - Structural demand for cost, access, and workforce solutions across provider and payer ecosystems

 - Readiness for workflow-native software that automates operational drudgery and elevates clinical work

 - Data liquidity and AI unlocking new products in revenue cycle, care coordination, capacity planning, and patient engagement

 - Enterprise partnerships that shorten the path from idea to pilot to scaled deployment




WHAT YOU GET AT TERRARIUM:

 - A rigorous research platform with primary insights, market maps, and analyst support

 - Customer access across health systems, payers, and employer buyers for rapid validation

 - Co-founder matching and recruiting support to assemble an elite early team

 - Builder services including product, design, data, and regulatory guidance

 - A clear funding pathway from pre-seed with milestones for follow-on capital




PROGRAM DETAILS:

 - Commitment: full time. 4 months, full-time with the goal of company formation

 - Location: New York City hybrid. We value in-person working sessions with flexib

notes: Small sample size, but strong tech focus in Founding Engineer role.
